methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Lodge Systems is a pre-seed stage company in Defense & Security founded by Stuart Lodge. The valuation universe consists of 10 comparable companies across the sector: 2 publicly traded (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. While 20 companies operate within Defense & Security broadly, filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage pre-seed companies yielded fewer than three datapoints, below the minimum threshold for reliable median-based valuation. An all-sector median approach would introduce material bias given the wide dispersion in defense tech risk profiles and commercialization timelines across stages.

The analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range at this time. Advancing a defensible range would require either: (1) expanding the peer set to include adjacent security-tech stages with disclosed valuations, (2) sourcing pitch-stage benchmarks specific to Defense & Security pre-seed cohorts, or (3) applying founder-backed financing comps if prior rounds are disclosed. The key uncertainty is stage-appropriate pricing in a sector where pre-seed multiples vary significantly based on IP maturity, regulatory pathway clarity, and founder defense experience. Recommend revisiting this analysis once additional comparables or transaction data surfaces.
